assertions.assertion: Response warns that the issuer URI (iss claim) must remain the same after migration
assertions.rationale: States 'the issuer URI must remain the same' and explains it appears as the 'iss claim in tokens.'

assertions.assertion: Response explains that changing the issuer breaks existing tokens and/or client trust relationships
assertions.rationale: States 'Existing tokens will fail validation because the iss claim won't match what APIs expect' and 'Refresh tokens may become invalid.'

assertions.assertion: Response recommends setting options.IssuerUri explicitly in the Duende IdentityServer configuration
Response suggests checking the current /.well-known/openid-configuration discovery document to find the existing issuer value
assertions.rationale: States 'Verify the discovery document' and 'check /.well-known/openid-configuration to confirm the issuer value matches the original.' However, this is framed as a post-migration verification step rather than a pre-migration step to find the current value. Still, /.well-known/openid-configuration is explicitly mentioned for checking the issuer.
